canonical_truth.summary: A large sheet of printed paper, carried by wind, caught in a branch fork above the root cross — dense with black markings, wrinkled at the edges, ink still dark.
canonical_truth.location: root cross area / east boundary territory
canonical_truth.concrete_outcome: YY stopped and looked at the page; each branch engaged with it differently depending on what they carried

perception_prompt.noticeable_surface: a large sheet of printed paper, caught high in a branch fork, stirring in the morning wind — dense with black markings he cannot read; it has been there long enough to wrinkle at the edges but the ink is still dark

authorial_note.real_world_inspiration: World Book Day / Talk Like Shakespeare Day (April 23) — UNESCO literacy observance; Shakespeare's 462nd birthday
authorial_note.translation_logic: The celebration of language and performance enters as a physical object — a page of text YY cannot decode — that triggers his randomly_eloquent trait; each branch addresses its own inventory object as witness, or the page directly
authorial_note.restraint_reason: kept the page as a found object, not an announcement; YY's response emerges from looking, not from understanding
